description: You are a mission analyst assigned to compute preliminary trajectory parameters for a 2026 Mars orbiter mission. Design a minimum-energy Type I Earth-to-Mars transfer trajectory using GMAT's heliocentric propagation and targeting capabilities, following the detailed specifications in ~/Desktop/mars_mission_requirements.txt. The mission requires a spacecraft in a 200 km circular Earth parking orbit (28.5 deg INC) and a Trans-Mars Injection (TMI) impulsive burn targeted for a Mars closest approach of less than 500,000 km using a DifferentialCorrector. Record the departure C3 (km^2/s^2), TMI DeltaV (km/s), time of flight (days), and closest approach distance (km) in ~/GMAT_output/mars_transfer_results.txt and save the final GMAT script to ~/GMAT_output/mars_transfer.script. Login: username=ga, password=password123
